summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Grep with ripgrep and native fallback
reasoning: Created Grep tool that uses ripgrep (rg) when available for performance, falling back to :re + File.stream when not installed. Used System.cmd/3 for rg execution with appropriate flags: --max-count 100 to cap matches, --glob for file filtering, --ignore-case for case-insensitive search, --context for context lines. Native fallback implements basic .gitignore support by rejecting common ignored directories (.git, node_modules, _build, deps). Extracted nested formatting logic into helper functions (format_match, format_context_line) to reduce nesting depth from 4 to 3, satisfying Credo's strict mode requirement. Both implementations cap results at 100 matches and respect .gitignore patterns.

summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Bash with Port-based execution and streaming
reasoning: Created Bash tool that spawns commands via Elixir Port for proper process management. Streams stdout/stderr in real-time to TUI via context.emit function. Configurable timeout (default 120s) with remaining time recalculated each receive loop to prevent timeout drift. Output truncation: first by bytes (30KB max), then by lines (100 max), handling trailing newlines correctly by separating line content from newline flag. Full output saved to temp file when truncated, with automatic cleanup of empty temp files. Shell escaping via single-quote wrapping with ''' replacement for embedded quotes. Created comprehensive test suite with 22 tests covering execution, streaming, timeouts, truncation, temp files, and shell escaping.

summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Edit with two modes and file scope enforcement
reasoning: Created Edit tool with two modes: (1) string-match mode requiring unique old_string match, returning unified diff; (2) line-range mode replacing specific line ranges. Both modes enforce file_scope when set. Refactored validation and replacement into separate functions (validate_line_range, perform_line_range_replacement) to reduce cyclomatic complexity from 10 to below 9 per credo requirements. Implemented helpful error messages with nearby text suggestions when string match fails. Created comprehensive test suite with 26 tests covering both modes, edge cases, and file scope enforcement.

summary: Implemented file scope check using path normalization and prefix matching
reasoning: Used Path.expand/1 to normalize paths and String.starts_with?/2 to check if a path is within the allowed scope. This approach naturally allows subdirectories within scoped paths while rejecting paths outside the scope.

summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Read with map-based media type lookup
reasoning: Created Read tool with file reading, pagination (offset/limit), line-numbered output, and image base64 encoding. Used module attribute map for media type lookup instead of case statement to satisfy Credo's cyclomatic complexity limit (max 9). Supports common image formats (.png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .bmp, .webp, .svg, .ico, .tiff). Line numbers are 1-indexed to match editor conventions. Returns Text content blocks per spec.

summary: Implemented Deft.Provider.Registry GenServer with register and resolve functions
reasoning: Created GenServer to store provider configs in a map (provider_name -> module). Implemented register/2 to add providers and resolve/2 to look up provider module + model config by calling the provider's model_config/1 callback. Returns {:ok, {module, config}} or {:error, :unknown_provider/:unknown_model}. Added comprehensive test suite with 10 tests covering registration, resolution of valid/invalid providers/models, and integration with real Anthropic provider.

summary: Implemented format_tools/1 to convert Deft.Tool modules to Anthropic tools format
reasoning: Mapped Tool behaviour callbacks (name/0, description/0, parameters/0) to Anthropic's tools format with name, description, and input_schema fields. Simple Enum.map over tool modules calling the three callbacks. Added 3 comprehensive tests: empty list, single tool conversion, and multiple tools. All tests pass.

summary: Defined Deft.Tool behaviour and Deft.Tool.Context struct
reasoning: Created the Tool behaviour module with 4 required callbacks: name/0 (returns tool name string), description/0 (returns description for system prompt), parameters/0 (returns JSON Schema for args), and execute/2 (runs the tool with args and context). Defined Tool.Context struct with working_dir, session_id, emit function, and optional file_scope for orchestration. Used @enforce_keys to ensure required fields are always provided. Added comprehensive test suite with 5 tests covering behaviour implementation, execution flow, context creation, and emit functionality.

summary: Implemented format_messages/1 with smart system parameter handling
reasoning: Converts Deft.Message structs to Anthropic wire format per spec section 4. System messages with only Text content are joined into a single string for the system parameter (cleaner API format). Mixed content types use array format. All 5 content block types (Text, ToolUse, ToolResult, Thinking, Image) map to their Anthropic equivalents. Added 11 comprehensive tests covering all scenarios including edge cases.

summary: Validated parse_event/1 with comprehensive tests
reasoning: The parse_event/1 implementation existed but lacked proper test coverage. Added 12 test cases covering all event types from spec section 4: content_block_start (text/thinking/tool_use), content_block_delta (text_delta/thinking_delta/input_json_delta), message_delta (usage), message_stop, error events, unknown events, and malformed data. All tests pass, confirming correct implementation of Anthropic SSE event mapping to common Deft event types.

summary: Refactored SSE parsing to use parse_event/1 callback
reasoning: Moved event parsing logic from inline handlers into the parse_event/1 callback per spec. Piped SSE events from ServerSentEvents.parse/1 through parse_event/1 to normalize into Deft events. Extracted event handling into separate functions (process_single_event, handle_parsed_event) to reduce nesting depth from 4 to 3, meeting credo max_nesting requirement. Tool call state accumulation remains in streaming layer since it requires stateful tracking across multiple events (start, deltas, stop).

summary: Implemented Deft.Provider.Anthropic.stream/3 and cancel_stream/1 with SSE parsing
reasoning: Created streaming HTTP client using Req with into: :self to receive chunks as messages. Spawned separate process to handle HTTP connection and SSE parsing using ServerSentEvents.parse/1. Implemented event routing to parse Anthropic-specific SSE events (content_block_start, content_block_delta, etc.) and send normalized provider events to caller via {:provider_event, event} messages. Implemented cancel_stream/1 to terminate stream process. Refactored event routing into multiple function clauses to keep cyclomatic complexity below 9 per credo requirements.

summary: Implemented Deft.Agent.SystemPrompt.build/1 with comprehensive system prompt
reasoning: Created SystemPrompt module that builds system prompt from role definition, tool descriptions (via Tool behaviour callbacks), environment info (working dir, git branch, date, OS), and conflict resolution rules. Handles missing tools gracefully. Structured as sectioned markdown for clarity. Updated Context.build_system_prompt to use new module instead of placeholder.

summary: Implemented Deft.Agent.Context.build/2 with project context file loading
reasoning: Assembled message list in spec-defined order: system prompt (placeholder for now), observation injection point (nil until OM implemented), conversation history, and project context from DEFT.md/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. Implemented file priority (DEFT.md > CLAUDE.md > AGENTS.md) and reference resolution (CLAUDE.md can point to AGENTS.md). Created comprehensive test suite covering all scenarios.

summary: Validated prompt queueing implementation with comprehensive tests
reasoning: The prompt queueing functionality was already implemented in the Agent code (lines 67, 183-188, 595-638). Created test suite to validate: (1) prompts sent while agent is not :idle are queued using :queue.new/in, (2) queued prompts are delivered on return to :idle via handle_idle_transition/1. Tests confirmed queueing works correctly. Also discovered a limitation in queue delivery from state enter callbacks that will need addressing in future work.

summary: Implemented turn limit with pause-and-ask pattern
reasoning: Added turn counter that increments on :executing_tools → :calling transition, resets on new user prompts. When max_turns (default 25) is reached, agent broadcasts turn_limit_reached event and stays in :executing_tools state. User can respond via continue_turn(agent, true/false) to either reset counter and continue or return to idle. This prevents runaway loops while allowing user control.

summary: Refactored tool execution to use Task.async for abortability
reasoning: Changed from synchronous execute_batch call to Task.async wrapper, storing task struct in state. This enables abort handler to call Task.shutdown on in-flight tasks. Also extracted helper functions to reduce cyclomatic complexity per credo requirements.

summary: Used multiple function clauses instead of case statement for streaming events
reasoning: Refactored streaming event handler to use multiple handle_event clauses (one per event type) instead of a single clause with case statement. This reduced cyclomatic complexity from 10 to 1 per clause, passing credo's max complexity of 9.

summary: Created Provider behaviour with 8 event types
reasoning: Implemented Deft.Provider behaviour module defining the 6 required callbacks (stream/3, cancel_stream/1, parse_event/1, format_messages/1, format_tools/1, model_config/1) and 8 event type structs (TextDelta, ThinkingDelta, ToolCallStart, ToolCallDelta, ToolCallDone, Usage, Done, Error) per spec. Event structs use defstruct with @enforce_keys to ensure required fields are always present.

summary: Organized ContentBlock types as separate nested modules
reasoning: Created Deft.Message.Text, Deft.Message.ToolUse, Deft.Message.ToolResult, Deft.Message.Thinking, and Deft.Message.Image as distinct modules under Deft.Message. This provides clear namespacing, better documentation per type, and makes the union type explicit via @type content_block. All structs enforce required keys for safety.

summary: Installed Elixir 1.19.5 and lefthook 2.1.4 via Homebrew
reasoning: The verification task required running make setup and make check, which depend on Elixir/Mix and lefthook. Installed both via Homebrew on macOS. Also ran mix format to fix inline comment formatting in mix.exs per Elixir formatter conventions.

summary: Created .credo.exs with strict mode and required checks
reasoning: Implemented Credo configuration per spec section 5 with strict: true and five key enabled checks (WithCustomTaggedTuple, MixEnv, AliasUsage, CyclomaticComplexity, Nesting with max_nesting: 3)

summary: Interactive AI session for issue creation, structured JSON storage
reasoning: Humans are bad at writing structured data, AIs are bad at parsing unstructured data. The creation session lets the user speak naturally while the AI extracts structure (context, acceptance_criteria, constraints). The Foreman reads clean JSON — no markdown conventions or heuristic parsing. --quick flag for trivial issues.

summary: Single approval for deft work --loop
reasoning: User approves the first job's plan to verify Deft's approach. Subsequent jobs auto-approve. Balances oversight with autonomy — user doesn't need to babysit but gets a chance to course-correct early.

summary: 90-day compaction for closed issues
reasoning: Keep the JSONL file focused on active work. Git history preserves full record. 90 days is long enough to reference recent completions, short enough to prevent unbounded growth. Configurable via issues.compaction_days.

summary: Build native issue tracker instead of integrating Seeds/Beads
reasoning: Seeds validates JSONL+git approach but is Bun/TypeScript — shelling out adds runtime dependency. Deft already has JSONL+GenServer+ETS patterns from Site Log. Native Elixir implementation fits OTP architecture. Take concepts (ready queue, hash IDs, merge=union, atomic writes) but implement in-house.

summary: JSONL+git over Dolt or SQLite for issue storage
reasoning: Dolt is heavyweight (Go binary dependency) and solves distributed sync Deft doesn't need. SQLite adds a dependency for sequential access patterns. JSONL is consistent with Site Log and sessions, human-readable, git-diffable. merge=union gitattribute handles branch merges. Scale is small (tens-hundreds of issues).

summary: Minimal v0.1: no labels, no assignees, no templates
reasoning: Priority + dependencies cover the core need (what to work on next). Labels, assignees, and templates add complexity not justified until issue count grows or multi-agent assignment is needed. All are additive — easy to add later without breaking changes.

summary: Chunk-based decomposition, not file-based
reasoning: Real work has overlapping files. Foreman plans deliverables (1-3 Leads), not implementation steps. Dependency DAG + git worktrees handle integration. File ownership was too rigid.

summary: Runners are inline loops, different from Leads/Foreman
reasoning: Tasks can't own supervision subtrees. Runners making 3-10 LLM calls don't need gen_statem or OM. Asymmetry between agent tiers is acceptable.

summary: Lead actively steers Runners, is the memory bridge
reasoning: Lead is more trustworthy than OM for Runner context. Lead reads Runner output, evaluates, course-corrects. Pair-programming manager model.

summary: Worktree per Lead, conflict resolver merges
reasoning: Each Lead gets isolated worktree. Whoever has the conflict resolves it at merge time. Foreman merges in dependency order.

summary: Partial dependency unblocking via interface contracts
reasoning: Lead B doesn't wait for all of Lead A — just needs the specific contract (API shape). Foreman watches Site Log for contract entries and unblocks immediately.

summary: Remove suggested-response from Observer output
reasoning: Cheap model should not steer expensive Actor. Dynamic continuation hint provides continuity without the steering risk.

summary: Keep 30k first-activation threshold
reasoning: Lowering to 8-10k for short sessions not worth the extra Observer cost. OM is for long sessions.

summary: Observation sectioning promoted to requirement
reasoning: 10-agent review consensus. Named sections (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History) improve LLM attention, user readability, and Reflector compression. Current State at top gets highest attention weight.

summary: Keep Breeze for TUI despite risk
reasoning: User wants the LiveView-style API. Mitigation: build a streaming proof-of-concept early to validate performance. Fallback to Termite + custom rendering if it fails.

summary: No security/permission layer in v0.1
reasoning: User decision: focus on core functionality first. Tool permissions, sandboxing, secret redaction deferred to future spec.

summary: YAML for config format, not TOML
reasoning: TOML has poor Elixir ecosystem support (unmaintained libraries). YAML via yaml_elixir is actively maintained. JSON also acceptable.

summary: Burrito from day 1 for single binary
reasoning: Single binary distribution is a hard requirement. Accept the build complexity from the start rather than deferring.

summary: 40k reflection threshold validated by Mastra testing
reasoning: Mastra has empirical data showing 40k observation tokens work well with LLM attention. Context Expert recommended lowering to 20-25k, but real-world testing overrides theoretical concern.

summary: Replaced Observer/Reflector GenServers with Task-based architecture
reasoning: 10-agent review identified that long-running LLM calls in GenServers is an OTP anti-pattern (blocks the process, makes it unresponsive). Tasks spawned by State are simpler, cancellable, and don't require persistent processes.

summary: Per-session scope only for v0.1, cross-session deferred
reasoning: Cross-session memory (Mastra's resource scope) requires merging observations across threads and adds complexity around deduplication and conflict resolution. Per-session is the core value prop and sufficient for v0.1.

summary: Character-based token estimation over tokenizer dependency
reasoning: A real tokenizer (tiktoken) adds a NIF or large dependency. The 4:1 char/token ratio is well-known and sufficient for threshold decisions. Calibration from actual provider usage reports improves accuracy over time without adding deps.

summary: No embeddings or retrieval — always-present observations
reasoning: Retrieval-augmented memory requires the agent to know what it doesn't know. OM takes the opposite approach: everything observed is always injected. Token cost is managed by two-level compression (observation + reflection), not by selective retrieval. This matches Mastra's proven design.

summary: Named the project Deft
reasoning: Short, memorable, not an existing CLI command on Linux/macOS. Fits naming style of user's other projects (moto, specd). No major GitHub/npm/hex conflicts. Emacs has an elpa-deft package but it does not install a CLI binary.

summary: Chose Elixir/OTP over Pi SDK wrapper or TypeScript
reasoning: User wants stability, tailored flows, and natural concurrency for OM. Elixir's process model maps perfectly to Observer/Reflector as independent processes. Pi SDK wrapper would create an upstream dependency risk the user wants to avoid.

summary: Chose Breeze (Termite stack) for TUI over raw ANSI or other frameworks
reasoning: Breeze provides LiveView-style API that Elixir developers know, built-in components for scrolling/layout, and is actively maintained by a Phoenix core team member. Drafter and TermUI are alternatives but less proven. Raw ANSI is too much work for a proper chat interface.

summary: Chose gen_statem over GenServer for agent loop
reasoning: Agent loop has distinct states (idle, calling, streaming, executing_tools) with explicit transitions. gen_statem makes invalid transitions impossible at the framework level. Pattern from Opal validated this approach.

summary: Chose JSONL over SQLite for session persistence
reasoning: Sessions are read linearly (rebuild from start to end), append-only during operation, and human-readable for debugging. SQLite would add a dependency and is overkill for sequential access patterns.

summary: Scoped out MCP, sub-agents, and IDE integrations to future phases
reasoning: Core value is OM + stability. MCP and sub-agents add complexity without contributing to the differentiator. IDE integrations are a separate concern entirely.

summary: Learned from Opal but chose not to depend on it
reasoning: Opal validates Elixir/OTP works for coding agents (gen_statem loop, supervised tools, SSE streaming). But it's Copilot-only, hobby project, single author, very new — the exact instability risk user wants to avoid. Patterns are reusable without the dependency.

summary: Created harness as foundation spec, OM as separate spec
reasoning: Harness (agent loop, tools, TUI, sessions) and OM (observer, reflector, context injection) are distinct concerns with a clean process boundary between them. Separate specs allow independent iteration and implementation.
